sourceMovie.title: The Ides of March
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Ides of March works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Vice
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Ides of March clicked for you, Vice (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ben-Hur
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Ides of March clicked for you, Ben-Hur (1959) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Ides of March?
faq.answer: Try Vice, Ben-Hur, The Sting—each shares DNA with The Ides of March while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Ides of March?
faq.answer: The Ides of March (2011) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Ides of March worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
